site_description: A comprehensive hyper-converged cloud infrastructure management platform for deploying and managing databases, applications, storage, and virtual machines.
error_message_400: Oops! That request looks a bit wonky. Mind double-checking your input?
error_message_401: Hold up! You'll need to show your digital ID to enter this exclusive club.
error_message_403: Sorry, this area is VIP only! You don't have the golden ticket for this resource.
error_message_404: Well, this is awkward... The page seems to have gone on vacation without telling us!
error_message_500: Yikes! Our servers are having a moment. Please give us a sec to get things back on track.
error_message_501: Sorry! This feature is still on the engineers' to-do list and hasn't been implemented yet. We'll get them a cup of coffee to hurry it along.
error_message_502: Our gateway is feeling a bit under the weather. We're nursing it back to health!
error_message_503: We're temporarily out of order, like a vending machine that ate your dollar. Try again soon!
compute_dashboard_description: Monitor and manage your compute resources including virtual machines and GPUs.
machine_dashboard_description: Monitor bare metal machine metrics including CPU, memory, storage, and GPU utilization.
machine_dashboard_nodes_tooltip: Displays the total number of nodes in the cluster over the past 6 months.
machine_dashboard_total_cpu_tooltip: Displays the current CPU usage and requested CPU resources as a percentage of total allocatable CPU.
machine_dashboard_total_memory_tooltip: Displays the current memory usage and requested memory resources as a percentage of total allocatable memory.
machine_dashboard_total_storage_tooltip: Displays the total storage capacity and usage.
machine_dashboard_gpu_tooltip: Displays the current GPU utilization as a percentage of total available GPU resources.
machine_dashboard_gpu_memory_tooltip: Displays the current GPU memory usage and requested GPU memory resources as a percentage of total allocatable GPU memory.
machine_dashboard_system_loads_tooltip: Displays the system load average over 1, 5, and 15 minutes.
under_development_description: Our elves are working their magic!
deletion_warning: Please type the {identifier} exactly to confirm deletion. This action cannot be undone.
storage_dashboard_description: Monitor Ceph storage cluster performance, capacity usage, health status, OSD distribution, and throughput analytics. View real-time metrics for cluster health, capacity trends, and storage operations.
gpu_allocation_model_description: LLMInferenceService → Pod → GPU → Node relationship diagram
gpu_allocation_empty_model: No GPU devices found for this model. Pods may not have HAMi vGPU allocations.
gpu_allocation_empty_node: No GPU devices registered on this node.
node_distribution_description: Distribution of physical and virtual nodes in the cluster.
llm_dashboard_description: Provides real-time insights into model performance, including end-to-end latency and time to first token.
llm_dashboard_requests_tooltip: Shows the 95th and 99th percentile request latency.
llm_dashboard_requests_waiting_tooltip: Running is baseline batch load. Persistent Waiting means undersized capacity (add replicas). Swapped > 0 means KV cache was preempted — worst UX, add replicas or tune max_num_seqs.
llm_dashboard_models_tooltip: A sudden drop usually means a model was deleted or its pods crashed.
llm_dashboard_throughputs_tooltip: Tokens processed per second, split into prompt (input) and generation (output). Prompt much higher than generation = input-heavy workload (e.g. RAG, long context). Generation higher = output-heavy (e.g. chat, long replies). Both flat while latency is high = the bottleneck isn't throughput.
llm_dashboard_time_to_first_token_toolti: How long a user waits before seeing the first response token. Sudden spikes usually mean prefill is overloaded — long prompts, full batch, or saturated KV cache. Check KV Cache and Active Requests alongside this chart.
llm_dashboard_time_per_output_token_tool: Time the model takes to generate each output token. Rising values mean decoding is slowing down, usually from GPU saturation or KV cache pressure.
llm_dashboard_kv_cache_tooltip: Per-pod KV cache usage — direct indicator of load balance health. Pods moving together means router is fine; one pod hot while others are cold means routing problem.
llm_dashboard_queue_depth_tooltip: Brief spikes are fine, but persistent non-zero values mean overload. Waiting counts requests not yet batched (add replicas). Swapped means KV cache was preempted (worst UX, add replicas or tune max_num_seqs).
llm_dashboard_success_rate_tooltip: Below 100% means failures are occurring; check Analytics for the underlying cause.
llm_dashboard_total_requests_tooltip: Shown when the failure metric is unavailable. Reflects workload volume, not health.
llm_dashboard_finish_reason_tooltip: stop is normal completion and should dominate. length means max_tokens was hit and the response was truncated. abort means the client cancelled, usually a timeout. Rising length or abort signals upstream issues — compare with TTFT and E2E Latency.
llm_dashboard_e2e_latency_tooltip: If TTFT is fine but this is high, decoding is the bottleneck. A large p99 vs p95 gap means long-tail requests, often from KV cache pressure on long generations.
llm_dashboard_vgpu_tooltip: If KV Cache is low but a node's GPU memory is high, another workload is competing for the GPU. If both move together, vLLM itself is the consumer.
llm_dashboard_time_to_first_token_descri: First-token latency at p95 and p99
llm_dashboard_time_per_output_token_desc: Inter-token latency at p95 and p99
llm_dashboard_e2e_latency_description: End-to-end request latency at p95 and p99
llm_dashboard_per_pod_kv_cache_descripti: Per-pod KV cache utilization over time
llm_dashboard_prompt_size_description: Request rate by prompt token length
llm_dashboard_generation_size_descriptio: Request rate by generation token length
llm_dashboard_top_throughput_description: Models with the highest token throughput
llm_dashboard_top_kv_pressure_descriptio: Models with the highest KV cache pressure
llm_dashboard_top_p99_latency_descriptio: Models with the worst p99 end-to-end latency
select_node_to_view_details: Select a node to view detailed metrics
select_namespace_to_view_details: Select a namespace to view detailed metrics
cluster_dashboard_node_pressure_descript: Per-node usage, request and limit vs allocatable
cluster_dashboard_node_pressure_tooltip: Usage is real consumption; Request is reserved capacity and drives scheduling; Limit is the ceiling — above 100% the node is over-committed. Rows are sorted so the most saturated node is on top.
node_cpu_pressure_tooltip: Each bar is one node's CPU request as a percentage of its allocatable cores; the limit percentage is shown alongside. Click a node to drill into its detail.
node_memory_pressure_tooltip: Each bar is one node's memory request as a percentage of its allocatable memory; the limit percentage is shown alongside. Click a node to drill into its detail.
node_pod_density_tooltip: Each bar is one node's running pod count as a percentage of its allocatable pods (default 110). A node can be pod-saturated without being CPU or memory bound. Click a node to drill into its detail.
node_gpu_utilization_tooltip: Each bar is one node's average compute utilization across its GPU cards (DCGM). Only GPU nodes appear; ranked busiest first so idle, expensive capacity stands out at the bottom. Click a node to drill into its per-card detail.
node_restart_count_tooltip: Total container restarts on each node over the last hour (kube-state-metrics joined to the node label). Only nodes with restarts are shown — an empty list means no recent restarts. Bars turn orange at 1–10 and red above 10 (sustained crash-looping). Click a node to drill into its detail.
namespace_cpu_usage_tooltip: Live CPU cores used per namespace (cAdvisor). Click a namespace to scope the pod detail below.
namespace_memory_usage_tooltip: Working-set memory used per namespace. Click a namespace to scope the pod detail below.
namespace_pod_count_tooltip: Running pods per namespace (kube-state-metrics). Click a namespace to scope the pod detail below.
namespace_restart_count_tooltip: Total container restarts per namespace over the last hour (kube-state-metrics). Only namespaces with restarts are shown — an empty list means no recent restarts. Bars turn orange at 1–10 and red above 10 (sustained crash-looping). Click a namespace to scope the pod detail below.
pvc_storage_usage_tooltip: Used capacity per PersistentVolumeClaim. Near 100% risks running out of space.
node_detail_cpu_tooltip: Non-idle CPU across all cores right now. Sustained highs mean the node is compute-bound.
node_detail_ram_tooltip: Memory in use as % of installed RAM. Above 90% may trigger OOM kills.
node_detail_swap_tooltip: How much swap is occupied. Any sustained swap usage on a Kubernetes node usually signals memory pressure and hurts latency. 0% is healthy.
node_detail_filesystem_tooltip: Used capacity of the root filesystem. Above 85% risks disk-pressure evictions.
node_detail_uptime_tooltip: How long this node has been part of the cluster, measured from its creation timestamp.
node_chart_cpu_core_tooltip: CPU time split by mode across all cores. High iowait signals a storage bottleneck; the stacked total is overall utilisation.
node_chart_ram_tooltip: Memory split into used, buffers, cache and free. Growing used with shrinking free is normal until cache can no longer be reclaimed — that is when pressure starts.
node_chart_disk_rw_tooltip: Bytes/s read from and written to disk; sustained highs with high I/O time mean a disk bottleneck.
node_chart_disk_io_tooltip: Share of time the disk was busy. Near 100% means it is saturated and requests queue.
node_chart_network_rx_tooltip: Bytes per second received per network interface. Compare with transmitted to spot asymmetric flows; the filter limits to the busiest interfaces.
node_chart_network_tx_tooltip: Bytes per second transmitted per network interface. Sudden sustained spikes can indicate large data transfers or replication traffic.
node_chart_gpu_util_tooltip: Per-card GPU compute utilization. Sustained highs mean the GPUs are well used; lows on a busy node suggest the bottleneck is elsewhere (data loading, CPU, network).
node_chart_gpu_memory_tooltip: Per-card GPU memory in use as a percentage of each card's framebuffer. Near 100% risks out-of-memory workloads.
node_chart_gpu_power_tooltip: Per-card power draw in watts. Tracks how hard each GPU is working and helps spot power or thermal ceilings.
node_chart_gpu_temp_tooltip: Per-card GPU temperature in °C. Sustained high temperatures can trigger thermal throttling and lower performance.
node_detail_pods_tooltip: Scheduled pods vs the node capacity. Near 100% no more pods fit, even with free CPU/memory.
node_chart_cpu_commit_tooltip: CPU as % of allocatable cores. Request drives scheduling; a Limit over 100% means the node is over-committed.
node_chart_mem_commit_tooltip: Memory as % of allocatable. Request drives scheduling; a Limit over 100% risks OOM eviction under pressure.
node_chart_network_io_tooltip: Bytes/s received and transmitted across all interfaces (excludes loopback).
pod_detail_table_tooltip: Current CPU, memory, restarts, age and network per pod, sorted to surface the most-restarted first.
workload_kpi_pods_tooltip: Running pods in the current scope.
workload_kpi_cpu_tooltip: Live CPU cores used by containers in scope.
workload_kpi_memory_tooltip: Live working-set memory used by containers in scope.
workload_kpi_restarts_tooltip: Container restarts in the last hour; a rising count points to unhealthy workloads.
ns_commit_cpu_tooltip: Namespace CPU in cores: Usage vs Request vs Limit. Request far above Usage wastes scheduling quota.
ns_commit_mem_tooltip: Namespace memory: Usage vs Request vs Limit. Usage near Limit risks OOM kills.
llm_dashboard_top_throughput_tooltip: Models processing the most tokens per second right now (prompt + generation combined). Compare with KV Pressure to tell heavy load from actual saturation.
llm_dashboard_top_kv_pressure_tooltip: Highest KV cache usage among each model's pods. Above 85% means requests can be preempted (worst user experience). Low throughput with high pressure means a long-context workload, not high traffic.
llm_dashboard_top_p99_latency_tooltip: Slowest 1% of requests per model — the worst experience users actually see. Models appearing on all three top charts should be tuned first.
llm_dashboard_active_requests_tooltip: Requests being processed (Running) vs. queued up (Waiting). Some Running is normal load. If Waiting stays above zero, capacity is undersized — add replicas. Brief spikes under heavy traffic are expected.
llm_dashboard_per_pod_kv_cache_tooltip: Each line is one pod's KV cache usage. Lines moving together = the router is balancing fairly (add replicas if they're all high). One pod high while others are low = traffic is being routed unevenly.
llm_dashboard_prefill_decode_tooltip: The two stages of inference: prefill processes the input prompt, decode generates the output. Prefill ≫ Decode = input is the bottleneck (raise tensor parallelism, enable prefix cache). Decode ≫ Prefill = output is the bottleneck (add replicas, tune max_num_seqs).
llm_dashboard_prefix_cache_tooltip: How often vLLM reuses cached prompt prefixes instead of recomputing them. High hit rate = TTFT stays low and throughput is good. Low hit rate = cache is too small, or prompts share little prefix (system prompt placed late, every user starts differently).
llm_dashboard_prompt_size_tooltip: Distribution of incoming prompt lengths — tells you what kind of workload this is. Concentrated short = chat. Concentrated long = RAG or document QA (raise max_model_len, enable prefix cache). Two peaks (short and long mixed) = consider splitting short and long traffic into separate deployments.
llm_dashboard_generation_size_tooltip: Distribution of response lengths. Concentrated short = classification or extraction (decode is not the bottleneck). Concentrated long = agent or writing tasks (decode-bound risk — watch TPOT and replica count).
llm_dashboard_per_replica_throughput_too: Tells you whether adding replicas actually helped. Per-pod throughput stays steady after scaling = each pod still has work (traffic is also growing). Per-pod drops = traffic is being split (scaling worked). Big gaps between pods = a load-balancing problem.
select_model_to_view_details: Select a model to view detailed metrics
push_image_description: Push an image to a project.
push_chart_description: Push a chart to a project.
model_response_error: Oops! Something went wrong while fetching the response. Please try again or check your network connection.
health_check_statment: Are you alive? Please respond if you can process this message.
model_testing_init: Welcome! I'm your AI assistant. How can I help you today?
trouble_pushing_image_step_one: 1. Locate or Create {daemon} and Add or Edit {insecure_registries}
trouble_pushing_image_step_one_details: Add or update the {insecure_registries} array in your {daemon} file (typically located at {path} on Linux) to include the registry address:
trouble_pushing_image_step_two_details: To restart the Docker daemon on Linux, run the following commands:
validate_rfc1123: Must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters, '-' or '.', and must start and end with an alphanumeric character.
validate_alphanum_dash: Must consist of alphanumeric characters or '-'.
validate_lower_alphanum_dash: Must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters or '-'.
validate_alphanum_dash_start_alpha: Must consist of alphanumeric characters or '-', and start with a letter.
validate_lower_alphanum_dash_start_alpha: Must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters or '-', and start with a letter.
validate_lower_alphanum_dash_dot: Must consist of lower case alphanumeric characters, '-' or '.', and can start with '.'.
validate_max_length: Must be at most {max} characters long.
console_description: Please select a cluster from the top-right menu to view your workspace dashboard.
console_guide_cluster_description: Start here by selecting your target Cluster environment to initialize the dashboard.
console_description_add_workspace: No workspace found in cluster {cluster}. Please create a new workspace to get started.
console_guide_workspace_description: Once a cluster is selected, use this dropdown to navigate between different Workspaces.
console_guide_sidebar_description: Access all core features and navigate through the platform using this sidebar.
k8s_overview_description: Monitor your Kubernetes cluster status and resources
workspace_dashboard_description: Resource quota usage, workload readiness, storage, and recent pod signals for this workspace namespace.
workspace_workload_health_description: Ready vs total for LLMInferenceService, Application, HelmRelease, and VirtualMachine.
workspace_pod_rollout_health_description: Live signals from kube-state-metrics: pod phases, container waits, workload replicas, PVCs, and jobs.
workspace_storage_description: PVC storage requests, total count, and bound vs pending PVCs in this namespace.
workspace_namespace_unresolved: Workspace namespace is not available yet. Check the Workspace status.
cluster_dashboard_gpu_power_usage_descri: Total GPU power draw across the cluster
cluster_dashboard_cpu_description: Usage, request and limit vs allocatable
cluster_dashboard_cpu_tooltip: Usage is real consumption; Request is reserved capacity and drives scheduling; Limit is the ceiling — above 100% the cluster is over-committed (CPU throttling risk).
cluster_dashboard_memory_description: Usage, request and limit vs allocatable
cluster_dashboard_memory_tooltip: Usage is real consumption; Request is reserved capacity and drives scheduling; Limit is the ceiling — above 100% the cluster is over-committed (OOM / eviction risk).
cluster_dashboard_gpu_memory_tooltip: GPU memory consumed (usage) and reserved (request) across the cluster, as a percentage of total GPU memory.
cluster_dashboard_gpu_utilization_toolti: Average compute utilization across all GPU cards. High utilization is healthy — it means the GPUs are doing work.
setting_up_environment: Setting up your environment, please wait...
install_rook_ceph_no_disk_after_retry: Still no disk labels after retries. Ensure node disks are labeled by the exporter (otterscale.io/disk.*).
install_rook_ceph_no_disk_this_node: No usable disks detected on this node.
import_cluster_administrators_descriptio: Selected users will be granted cluster-admin via ClusterRoleBinding.
import_cluster_no_administrators_descrip: Grant trusted users cluster-admin access to manage this cluster.
import_cluster_deploy_agent_description: Run the generated command on your target cluster to enroll it.
import_cluster_install_command_descripti: Copy and execute this command with kubectl on the cluster you want to import.
import_cluster_managed_ready_suffix: is now managed and ready to use.
no_gpu_for_model: No GPU detected for this model
llm_dashboard_pod_hardware_status_toolti: Pod & GPU health at a glance: Restart / OOM / XID (24h), CPU Throttle (5m), and Shm (SIGBUS) crashes. Look into anything orange or red before tuning serving params.
llm_dashboard_cpu_throttle_hint: Share of CPU periods that were throttled (last 5 min). Sustained throttling slows tokenizer and request handling, indirectly raising TTFT — raise the CPU limit if this stays above 25%.
llm_dashboard_shm_crash_hint: Counts pods whose last crash was exit 135 (SIGBUS) — typically a full /dev/shm, which vLLM uses for engine↔worker IPC (the default is only 64 MB). Fix by enlarging it (emptyDir medium: Memory / --shm-size). Heuristic — SIGBUS has other causes too, and OOMKilled is a separate memory-limit issue.
llm_dashboard_memory_usage_no_limit_bann: No memory limit set — showing absolute GiB
llm_dashboard_memory_usage_tooltip: >90% = OOMKill risk; 70–90% = tight. When Pod Health shows OOM > 0, use this chart to find the affected pod.
llm_dashboard_gpu_memory_tooltip: >95% = node full (add GPUs or replicas); 80–95% = warm; <80% = vLLM over-reserved KV. Counts all workloads on the node, not just this model.
license_description: Manage and monitor the OtterScale cluster license.
license_no_license_description: No cluster license has been installed yet. Upload a license file to activate your cluster.
license_upload_description: Upload a .lic license file to activate your cluster.
license_upload_drop: Drag and drop your .lic file here, or click to browse
license_adjust_nodes_description: Override the automatic FIFO selection by specifying which nodes should be authorized. The list must not exceed the max node quota and must not violate GPU quota constraints.
license_adjust_nodes_empty: No override set. Nodes are authorized by FIFO order.
license_export_description: Export a license request file (.lreq) to submit to OtterScale for license renewal or upgrade.
license_export_no_data: No export data available. Click "Generate Export" to collect the fingerprint.
license_replace_description: Replace the current license token with a new .lic file.
license_export_generated: License request file has been generated. Click the download button.
license_export_upload_step: Step 2: Upload the .lic license file returned by the vendor
license_export_upload_drop: Drag and drop or click to select .lic file
license_fingerprint_provisional: Provisional license does not verify fingerprint
license_provisional_banner_title: Complete the official license application before expiry
license_provisional_banner_desc: Currently using a provisional license with limited features. Please follow the steps below to apply for an official license.
license_provisional_step1: Click "Export License Request" in the top-right, generate and download the .lreq file
license_provisional_step2: Submit the .lreq to the vendor through official channels for signing
license_provisional_step3: After review, the vendor will provide an official .lic license file
license_provisional_step4: After receiving the .lic, click "Import License" in the top-right to complete the upgrade
license_upload_missing_fields: Please provide a license name and select a .lic file
license_adjust_nodes_override_rejected: Node override was rejected due to quota violation. Nodes have reverted to FIFO order.
license_adjust_nodes_fifo_hint: FIFO mode: nodes are authorized in the order they joined the cluster.
license_adjust_nodes_readonly: This license has expired or is invalid. Node adjustments are not allowed.
